P001.name: Margaret Chen
P001.chief_complaint: Increased thirst and frequent urination for 3 weeks

P002.name: Robert Williams
P002.chief_complaint: Worsening shortness of breath and ankle swelling for 2 weeks

social.smoking: Former, quit 10 years ago, 30 pack-year history

P003.name: David Thompson
P003.chief_complaint: Worsening cough with sputum production and breathlessness for 5 days

P004.name: Sarah Martinez
P004.chief_complaint: Recurrent wheezing and chest tightness, especially at night

social.exercise: Yoga 3x/week, avoids intense cardio due to asthma

P005.name: James O'Brien
P005.chief_complaint: Persistent low mood, loss of interest in activities, and insomnia for 2 months

P006.name: Patricia Johnson
P006.chief_complaint: Chronic fatigue, weight gain, and constipation for 3 months

P007.name: William Davis
P007.chief_complaint: Bilateral knee pain worsening over 6 months, difficulty climbing stairs

P008.name: Linda Garcia
P008.chief_complaint: Burning epigastric pain worse after meals and at night for 4 weeks

P009.name: Dorothy Wilson
P009.chief_complaint: Fatigue, pallor, and heavy menstrual periods for 4 months

P010.name: Thomas Anderson
P010.chief_complaint: Productive cough with yellow-green sputum, fever, and chills for 3 days

social.smoking: Former, quit 3 years ago, 20 pack-year history

P011.name: Karen Lopez
P011.chief_complaint: Painful urination, frequency, and urgency for 2 days

P012.name: Jessica Rivera
P012.chief_complaint: Severe throbbing headache with nausea and light sensitivity for 2 days

P013.name: Harold Wright
P013.chief_complaint: Intermittent palpitations and dizziness for 1 week

P014.name: Richard Kim
P014.chief_complaint: Foamy urine and bilateral leg swelling for 3 weeks

P015.name: Angela Brooks
P015.chief_complaint: Unintentional weight loss of 15 lbs, fatigue, and night sweats for 6 weeks

social.exercise: Previously active, now limited by fatigue

P001.interpretation: Poorly controlled diabetes, above target of <7%
P001.notes: Increased from 7.8% six months ago. Consider medication adjustment.

P001.interpretation: Elevated fasting glucose consistent with uncontrolled diabetes. Renal function normal.
Elevated LDL, low HDL, elevated triglycerides. Suboptimal lipid control despite statin therapy.
P001.notes: Consider increasing statin dose or adding ezetimibe.

P002.interpretation: Significantly elevated, consistent with decompensated heart failure
P002.notes: Baseline BNP was 180 at last stable visit.

P002.interpretation: Hyponatremia and elevated potassium concerning in CHF. Elevated BUN/Cr suggesting prerenal azotemia from poor cardiac output. Hyperglycemia.
P002.notes: Monitor potassium closely given ACE inhibitor use.

P002.interpretation: INR within therapeutic range (2.0-3.0) for atrial fibrillation
Mild anemia, otherwise within normal limits

P003.interpretation: Elevated WBC suggesting infection or COPD exacerbation. Polycythemia consistent with chronic hypoxemia.
Elevated CO2 consistent with chronic CO2 retention in COPD. Low chloride from compensatory metabolic alkalosis.

P006.interpretation: Elevated TSH with low Free T4 and Free T3 indicating inadequately treated hypothyroidism. Elevated TPO antibodies confirm Hashimoto's thyroiditis.
P006.notes: Levothyroxine dose needs adjustment. Recheck in 6-8 weeks after dose increase.

P006.interpretation: Elevated LDL and triglycerides. Dyslipidemia may be worsened by undertreated hypothyroidism.
P006.notes: Optimize thyroid replacement before increasing statin dose.

P009.interpretation: Microcytic hypochromic anemia with elevated RDW, consistent with iron deficiency anemia. Reactive thrombocytosis.
Low serum iron, elevated TIBC, very low ferritin and transferrin saturation — classic iron deficiency anemia pattern.
P009.notes: Severe iron deficiency. Consider IV iron if oral supplementation inadequate.

P010.interpretation: Leukocytosis with left shift consistent with bacterial pneumonia.
Mildly low CO2 may reflect compensatory hyperventilation. Elevated glucose likely stress hyperglycemia plus known diabetes.
Markedly elevated inflammatory markers consistent with active infection.

P011.interpretation: Urinalysis consistent with acute bacterial urinary tract infection. Positive leukocyte esterase, nitrites, and significant bacteriuria.
P011.notes: Urine culture sent. Empiric antibiotics started.

P013.interpretation: Normal coagulation. Note: Patient is on apixaban which does not reliably affect standard coagulation tests.
P013.notes: Anti-Xa level may be considered if assessing apixaban effect.

P013.interpretation: All values within normal limits. Renal function adequate for apixaban dosing.
Normal TSH, thyroid dysfunction not a contributing factor to atrial fibrillation.

P014.interpretation: Elevated BUN and creatinine consistent with CKD Stage 3b (eGFR ~18 mL/min). Hyperkalemia, metabolic acidosis (low CO2), and hypocalcemia are complications of advanced CKD. Hyperglycemia present.
P014.notes: eGFR has declined from 32 to approximately 18 over 6 months. Nephrology follow-up recommended.

P014.interpretation: Normocytic anemia consistent with anemia of chronic kidney disease (decreased erythropoietin production).
P014.notes: Currently on epoetin alfa. Consider dose adjustment.

P014.interpretation: Significant proteinuria (3+) consistent with diabetic nephropathy. Glucosuria from hyperglycemia. Fixed specific gravity concerning for decreased concentrating ability.
P014.notes: 24-hour urine protein or spot urine protein/creatinine ratio recommended.

P014.interpretation: Suboptimally controlled diabetes. Note HbA1c may be falsely low in CKD due to reduced RBC lifespan.

P015.interpretation: Pancytopenia with microcytic anemia. Leukopenia and thrombocytopenia raise concern for bone marrow pathology given constitutional symptoms (weight loss, night sweats, fever).
P015.notes: Consider peripheral blood smear, LDH, and possible bone marrow biopsy.

P015.interpretation: Hypercalcemia and elevated total protein with low albumin suggest elevated globulins — consider lymphoproliferative disorder. Elevated alkaline phosphatase may indicate bone involvement.
P015.notes: Serum protein electrophoresis, LDH, uric acid, and CT imaging recommended.

P015.interpretation: Markedly elevated inflammatory markers. In the context of B symptoms (fever, weight loss, night sweats), lymphoma must be considered.

P007.interpretation: Mildly elevated inflammatory markers, consistent with osteoarthritis flare.
All values within normal limits. Renal function normal despite NSAID use.
P007.notes: Monitor renal function periodically given chronic celecoxib use.

P002.findings: Cardiomegaly with cardiothoracic ratio of 0.62. Bilateral pleural effusions, small to moderate on right, small on left. Pulmonary vascular congestion with cephalization of vessels. No consolidation or pneumothorax.
P002.impression: Findings consistent with decompensated congestive heart failure with bilateral pleural effusions and pulmonary edema.

P003.findings: Hyperinflated lungs with flattened diaphragms. Increased AP diameter. No acute infiltrate or consolidation. No pleural effusion. Emphysematous changes predominantly in upper lobes.
P003.impression: Chronic changes of COPD/emphysema without acute superimposed process.

P007.findings: Bilateral medial compartment joint space narrowing, more prominent on the right. Subchondral sclerosis and osteophyte formation at medial tibial plateau bilaterally. Small right knee joint effusion. No fracture or dislocation.
P007.impression: Moderate to severe bilateral medial compartment osteoarthritis, right worse than left.

P010.findings: Right lower lobe consolidation with air bronchograms. Blunting of the right costophrenic angle suggesting small right pleural effusion. No pneumothorax. Heart size normal.
P010.impression: Right lower lobe pneumonia with associated small parapneumonic effusion.

P014.findings: Both kidneys are small, measuring 8.5 cm (right) and 8.8 cm (left). Increased cortical echogenicity bilaterally with poor corticomedullary differentiation. No hydronephrosis, renal calculi, or mass. Left arm AV fistula with appropriate flow.
P014.impression: Bilateral small echogenic kidneys consistent with chronic kidney disease. No obstruction.

P015.findings: Multiple enlarged lymph nodes in the mediastinum (largest 3.2 cm), bilateral axillae, and retroperitoneum. Splenomegaly measuring 16.2 cm. No hepatomegaly. No pulmonary nodules or consolidation. No bowel obstruction.
P015.impression: Diffuse lymphadenopathy and splenomegaly. Findings highly suspicious for lymphoproliferative disorder. Recommend tissue biopsy for definitive diagnosis.

P013.findings: Left atrial enlargement (4.8 cm). Normal LV size with preserved systolic function (LVEF 58%). Mild mitral regurgitation. Mild tricuspid regurgitation with estimated RVSP 32 mmHg. No pericardial effusion. Diastolic dysfunction Grade I.
P013.impression: Left atrial enlargement consistent with atrial fibrillation. Preserved LV systolic function. Mild valvular regurgitation. Grade I diastolic dysfunction.

carvedilol.drug_class: Non-selective Beta-blocker with Alpha-1 blocking activity
contraindications: Decompensated heart failure requiring IV inotropes

warfarin.typical_dose: Individualized, typically 2-10mg daily, adjusted by INR

indications: Stroke prevention in hypertension with LVH

serious_side_effects: Suicidal ideation (black box warning in <25 yr)
Neuropsychiatric events (black box warning: mood changes, suicidal ideation)

contraindications: Known hypersensitivity to atropine or its derivatives
tiotropium.typical_dose: 18 mcg inhaled daily (HandiHaler) or 2.5 mcg/actuation 2 puffs daily (Respimat)

insulin_glargine.typical_dose: Individualized; typical starting 10 units at bedtime

common_side_effects: Generally well tolerated at recommended doses

contraindications: Personal or family history of medullary thyroid carcinoma
semaglutide.typical_dose: 0.25-1mg SC weekly (Ozempic); 3-14mg PO daily (Rybelsus); 2.4mg SC weekly (Wegovy)

contraindications: Pure red cell aplasia from prior ESA use
serious_side_effects: Increased mortality in cancer patients (if Hgb >11)

contraindications: Known hypersensitivity to cetirizine or hydroxyzine

indications: Nausea and vomiting (chemotherapy-induced, post-operative, acute gastroenteritis)

contraindications: History of pancreatitis with DPP-4 inhibitors

Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus.description: A chronic metabolic disorder characterized by insulin resistance and relative insulin deficiency, resulting in hyperglycemia.

Hypertension.description: Persistently elevated arterial blood pressure, defined as systolic >=130 mmHg and/or diastolic >=80 mmHg on repeated measurements.
diagnostic_criteria: SBP >=130 or DBP >=80 on 2+ separate occasions

COPD.description: A chronic inflammatory lung disease causing obstructed airflow from the lungs, encompassing emphysema and chronic bronchitis.

Asthma.description: A chronic inflammatory disorder of the airways characterized by variable airflow obstruction and bronchial hyperresponsiveness.
typical_symptoms: Cough (especially at night or early morning)
Symptoms triggered by allergens, exercise, or cold air

Major Depressive Disorder.description: A mood disorder characterized by persistent feelings of sadness, hopelessness, and loss of interest or pleasure in activities, with significant functional impairment.
typical_symptoms: Depressed mood most of the day
diagnostic_criteria: 5 or more symptoms during same 2-week period (must include depressed mood or anhedonia)
Symptoms cause significant distress or functional impairment
Not attributable to substance use or medical condition

Heart Failure.description: A clinical syndrome resulting from structural or functional cardiac disorders that impair the ability of the ventricle to fill or eject blood.
diagnostic_criteria: Framingham criteria (2 major or 1 major + 2 minor)
Elevated BNP (>100 pg/mL) or NT-proBNP (>300 pg/mL)
Echocardiographic evidence of systolic or diastolic dysfunction

Chronic Kidney Disease.description: Progressive loss of kidney function over months to years, classified by GFR and albuminuria categories.
diagnostic_criteria: Kidney damage markers (albuminuria, urine sediment abnormalities, electrolyte abnormalities) for >=3 months

Hypothyroidism.description: A condition in which the thyroid gland does not produce enough thyroid hormone, leading to decreased metabolic activity.
diagnostic_criteria: Elevated TSH with normal Free T4 = subclinical hypothyroidism

Osteoarthritis.description: A degenerative joint disease characterized by progressive loss of articular cartilage, subchondral bone changes, and osteophyte formation.
diagnostic_criteria: Clinical: age >50, stiffness <30 min, crepitus, bony tenderness
Radiographic: joint space narrowing, osteophytes, subchondral sclerosis, subchondral cysts

GERD.description: A condition in which gastric acid frequently flows back into the esophagus, causing symptoms and/or mucosal damage.
diagnostic_criteria: Typical symptoms responding to PPI trial
Positive symptom association on pH-impedance testing

Iron Deficiency Anemia.description: Anemia resulting from insufficient iron to support normal red blood cell production, the most common cause of anemia worldwide.

Community-Acquired Pneumonia.description: An acute infection of the pulmonary parenchyma acquired outside of healthcare settings.
diagnostic_criteria: Plus clinical findings: fever, purulent sputum, leukocytosis, hypoxemia
CURB-65 or PSI for severity assessment

Urinary Tract Infection.description: An infection involving any part of the urinary system, most commonly the bladder (cystitis) or kidneys (pyelonephritis).

Migraine with Aura.description: A primary headache disorder characterized by recurrent moderate to severe headaches with associated visual, sensory, or language disturbances preceding the headache.
diagnostic_criteria: Aura: fully reversible visual, sensory, or speech symptoms
At least 2 of: unilateral spread, each symptom lasts 5-60 min, at least 1 positive symptom, aura accompanied by headache within 60 min

Atrial Fibrillation.description: A supraventricular tachyarrhythmia with uncoordinated atrial electrical activation leading to ineffective atrial contraction and irregular ventricular response.
diagnostic_criteria: ECG showing: irregular R-R intervals, absence of distinct P waves, irregular atrial activity
Duration: paroxysmal (<7 days), persistent (>7 days), permanent

Hyperlipidemia.description: Abnormally elevated levels of lipids or lipoproteins in the blood, a major modifiable risk factor for atherosclerotic cardiovascular disease.

Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus.description: An autoimmune disorder resulting in destruction of pancreatic beta cells, leading to absolute insulin deficiency.

Coronary Artery Disease.description: Atherosclerotic disease of the coronary arteries, leading to reduced myocardial perfusion.

Generalized Anxiety Disorder.description: A mental health disorder characterized by persistent, excessive worry about various aspects of life that is difficult to control.
diagnostic_criteria: Excessive anxiety/worry occurring more days than not for >=6 months
3+ of: restlessness, fatigue, concentration difficulty, irritability, muscle tension, sleep disturbance

Benign Prostatic Hyperplasia.description: Non-malignant enlargement of the prostate gland causing lower urinary tract symptoms due to bladder outlet obstruction.
diagnostic_criteria: Decreased peak urinary flow rate (<10 mL/s)

Allergic Rhinitis.description: IgE-mediated inflammation of the nasal mucosa caused by exposure to allergens, resulting in nasal congestion, rhinorrhea, sneezing, and pruritus.

Obstructive Sleep Apnea.description: A sleep disorder characterized by repetitive upper airway obstruction during sleep, causing intermittent hypoxemia and sleep fragmentation.
diagnostic_criteria: Polysomnography: AHI >=5 with symptoms or AHI >=15 regardless of symptoms
Home sleep apnea testing can be used for high pretest probability

Chronic Bronchitis.description: A form of COPD defined by a productive cough lasting at least 3 months per year for 2 or more consecutive years.
diagnostic_criteria: Productive cough for >=3 months/year for >=2 consecutive years
Exclusion of other causes of chronic cough
Often associated with airflow limitation on spirometry

Osteoporosis.description: A systemic skeletal disorder characterized by low bone mass and microarchitectural deterioration, leading to increased bone fragility and fracture risk.
diagnostic_criteria: DXA T-score <=-2.5 at spine, hip, or forearm
FRAX score indicating high fracture risk

Gout.description: A form of inflammatory arthritis caused by deposition of monosodium urate crystals in joints and tissues due to hyperuricemia.
diagnostic_criteria: Identification of monosodium urate crystals in synovial fluid (gold standard)
Serum uric acid >6.8 mg/dL (may be normal during acute flare)
Clinical presentation with rapid onset monoarthritis

Peptic Ulcer Disease.description: A defect in the gastric or duodenal mucosa extending through the muscularis mucosae, most commonly caused by H. pylori or NSAID use.
typical_symptoms: Pain worse with meals (gastric) or relieved by meals (duodenal)
diagnostic_criteria: H. pylori testing (stool antigen, urea breath test, or biopsy)
Clinical symptoms responsive to acid suppression

Deep Vein Thrombosis.description: Formation of a blood clot in a deep vein, most commonly in the lower extremities.

Pulmonary Embolism.description: Obstruction of the pulmonary arterial vasculature by thrombus, most often originating from deep veins of the lower extremities.

Acute Myocardial Infarction.description: Myocardial necrosis resulting from acute obstruction of a coronary artery.
typical_symptoms: Radiation to left arm, jaw, back
diagnostic_criteria: Rise and/or fall of cardiac troponin with at least one value above 99th percentile
Plus: symptoms of ischemia, new ST-T changes, new Q waves, imaging evidence of new loss of viable myocardium
complications: Mechanical complications (papillary muscle rupture, ventricular septal defect)

Stroke.description: Acute neurological deficit caused by cerebrovascular ischemia (ischemic stroke) or hemorrhage (hemorrhagic stroke).

Rheumatoid Arthritis.description: A chronic autoimmune inflammatory disease primarily affecting the synovial joints, leading to progressive joint destruction.
diagnostic_criteria: Joint involvement, serology (RF, anti-CCP), acute phase reactants (ESR, CRP), duration >=6 weeks

Cellulitis.description: An acute bacterial skin infection involving the dermis and subcutaneous tissue, most commonly caused by streptococci or staphylococci.
diagnostic_criteria: Clinical diagnosis based on classic findings
Imaging to rule out abscess if fluctuant

Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease Ex.description: An acute worsening of respiratory symptoms in COPD that results in additional therapy, often triggered by infections or environmental factors.
diagnostic_criteria: Acute worsening of respiratory symptoms beyond normal day-to-day variations
Change in baseline dyspnea, cough, and/or sputum that warrants a change in management

Diabetic Nephropathy.description: Kidney damage resulting from diabetes mellitus, the leading cause of end-stage renal disease.
diagnostic_criteria: Persistent albuminuria (>30 mg/g creatinine) on 2 of 3 specimens over 3-6 months
In the setting of diabetes >=10 years (Type 1) or at diagnosis (Type 2)

Pneumothorax.description: Presence of air in the pleural space, which may occur spontaneously or secondary to trauma or lung disease.
typical_symptoms: Decreased breath sounds on affected side
diagnostic_criteria: Chest X-ray showing visceral pleural line without lung markings peripherally
CT chest (more sensitive for small pneumothorax)

Hypothermia.description: Core body temperature below 35°C (95°F), which can be mild (32-35°C), moderate (28-32°C), or severe (<28°C).

Sepsis.description: Life-threatening organ dysfunction caused by a dysregulated host response to infection.
diagnostic_criteria: Suspected or documented infection PLUS acute increase in SOFA score >=2 points
qSOFA >=2 (altered mentation, SBP <=100, RR >=22) for bedside screening

Acute Kidney Injury.description: A rapid decline in renal function occurring over hours to days, characterized by accumulation of waste products and fluid/electrolyte imbalances.
diagnostic_criteria: KDIGO criteria: increase in SCr >=0.3 mg/dL within 48h, or increase >=1.5x baseline within 7 days, or urine output <0.5 mL/kg/h for 6h

Pancreatitis.description: Inflammation of the pancreas, most commonly caused by gallstones or alcohol abuse.
typical_symptoms: Severe epigastric pain radiating to back
diagnostic_criteria: 2 of 3: characteristic abdominal pain, lipase/amylase >3x upper normal, imaging findings consistent with pancreatitis

Cirrhosis.description: End-stage liver disease characterized by diffuse hepatic fibrosis with replacement of normal architecture by regenerative nodules.
diagnostic_criteria: Laboratory (low albumin, elevated INR, elevated bilirubin)
Imaging showing nodular liver, splenomegaly, ascites

Epilepsy.description: A neurological disorder characterized by recurrent, unprovoked seizures due to abnormal excessive brain electrical activity.
diagnostic_criteria: 1 unprovoked seizure with high recurrence risk (>60%)

Anaphylaxis.description: A severe, potentially life-threatening systemic allergic reaction with rapid onset, involving multiple organ systems.
diagnostic_criteria: Acute onset (minutes to hours) with involvement of skin/mucosal tissue PLUS respiratory compromise and/or reduced BP
Or: 2+ organ systems involved after exposure to likely allergen

Peripheral Artery Disease.description: Atherosclerotic occlusive disease of the lower extremities, causing reduced blood flow to the legs.
diagnostic_criteria: CTA or MRA of lower extremities

Thyroid Cancer.description: Malignant neoplasm of the thyroid gland; papillary thyroid cancer is the most common type, accounting for 80-85% of cases.

Non-Hodgkin Lymphoma.description: A heterogeneous group of lymphoproliferative malignancies originating from B cells, T cells, or NK cells.
typical_symptoms: B symptoms (fever, night sweats, weight loss >10%)
diagnostic_criteria: Excisional lymph node biopsy with immunohistochemistry

Vitamin D Deficiency.description: Insufficient levels of vitamin D, defined as 25(OH)D <20 ng/mL, which can lead to bone disorders and has been associated with various systemic effects.

Insomnia.description: A sleep disorder characterized by difficulty initiating or maintaining sleep, or early morning awakening, with associated daytime impairment.
diagnostic_criteria: Not better explained by another sleep disorder

Menorrhagia.description: Abnormally heavy or prolonged menstrual bleeding (>80 mL per cycle or >7 days), a common gynecological complaint.
diagnostic_criteria: Clinical assessment of menstrual blood loss
Pelvic ultrasound to evaluate structural causes
Endometrial biopsy if age >45 or risk factors

Chronic Pain Syndrome.description: Pain persisting beyond the normal time of healing (typically >3 months) that is associated with significant emotional distress or functional disability.
diagnostic_criteria: Pain associated with significant emotional distress
Pain associated with significant functional disability
Cannot be attributed entirely to another condition

Uterine Fibroids.description: Benign smooth muscle tumors of the uterus (leiomyomas), the most common pelvic tumors in women.
diagnostic_criteria: Pelvic ultrasound showing well-defined uterine masses
Clinical examination with enlarged, irregular uterus

xray_chest.description: Posteroanterior and lateral radiograph of the chest to evaluate cardiopulmonary structures.

xray_knee.description: AP and lateral radiographs of the knee joint to evaluate bony structures and joint space.

ct_chest.description: Computed tomography of the chest with or without IV contrast for detailed cross-sectional imaging of thoracic structures.

ct_abdomen_pelvis.description: Computed tomography of the abdomen and pelvis with or without IV contrast.

ct_head.description: Non-contrast computed tomography of the head for rapid evaluation of intracranial pathology.

mri_brain.description: Magnetic resonance imaging of the brain with or without gadolinium contrast for detailed evaluation of brain parenchyma.

mri_spine.description: MRI of the cervical, thoracic, or lumbar spine for evaluation of spinal cord, nerve roots, and surrounding structures.

ultrasound_kidney.description: Renal ultrasound to evaluate kidney size, parenchymal echogenicity, hydronephrosis, and masses.

ultrasound_abdomen.description: Abdominal ultrasound to evaluate liver, gallbladder, pancreas, spleen, and kidneys.

echocardiogram.description: Transthoracic echocardiogram (TTE) using ultrasound to evaluate cardiac structure and function.

ct_pulmonary_angiography.description: CT angiography of the pulmonary arteries with IV contrast for detection of pulmonary embolism.
typical_indications: Acute dyspnea with pleuritic chest pain
Elevated D-dimer with high clinical suspicion

dexa_scan.description: Dual-energy X-ray absorptiometry for bone mineral density measurement.

second_line: Add GLP-1 receptor agonist (semaglutide, liraglutide) if ASCVD or CKD
Add SGLT2 inhibitor (empagliflozin, dapagliflozin) if heart failure or CKD
Add sulfonylurea or DPP-4 inhibitor if cost is a barrier
Insulin therapy if HbA1c significantly above target
monitoring: HbA1c every 3 months until at goal, then every 6 months
referral_criteria: Endocrinology: complex insulin regimen, recurrent hypoglycemia
red_flags: DKA (nausea, vomiting, abdominal pain, fruity breath, altered mental status)

first_line: ACE inhibitor or ARB (preferred in diabetes/CKD)
second_line: Beta-blocker (if compelling indication: HF, post-MI, rate control)
monitoring: Basic metabolic panel 1-2 weeks after starting/changing ACE/ARB/diuretic
referral_criteria: Resistant hypertension (uncontrolled on 3 drugs including diuretic)
Suspected secondary hypertension (young onset, sudden onset, hypokalemia)

first_line: ACE inhibitor or ARB (or ARNI: sacubitril/valsartan)
SGLT2 inhibitor (dapagliflozin or empagliflozin) for HFrEF
second_line: Ivabradine if HR >70 despite max beta-blocker
monitoring: Renal function and electrolytes (especially K+ on ACE/ARB/MRA)
Echocardiogram annually or with clinical change
referral_criteria: Cardiology: all new diagnoses, LVEF <40%, refractory symptoms
Advanced HF specialist: consideration for LVAD or transplant
Palliative care: refractory NYHA Class IV
red_flags: Acute decompensation (worsening dyspnea, rapid weight gain >3 lbs/day)
Cardiogenic shock (hypotension, cold extremities, altered mental status)

first_line: ICS/LABA combination if frequent exacerbations and eosinophils >300
second_line: Roflumilast for frequent exacerbations with chronic bronchitis phenotype
Supplemental oxygen if PaO2 <55 or SpO2 <88%
referral_criteria: Pulmonology: diagnostic uncertainty, severe COPD, frequent exacerbations
Surgical: lung volume reduction surgery or transplant evaluation

first_line: Step 2: Low-dose ICS daily + SABA PRN
second_line: Biologic therapy (omalizumab, mepolizumab, dupilumab) for severe asthma
Short course oral corticosteroids for exacerbations
lifestyle_modifications: Smoking cessation and avoidance of secondhand smoke
monitoring: Asthma Control Test (ACT) at each visit
Review inhaler technique at each visit
Monitor for side effects of ICS

second_line: Bupropion (especially if fatigue, sexual dysfunction, or smoking cessation needed)
Mirtazapine (especially if insomnia and poor appetite)
Augmentation with atypical antipsychotic or lithium
monitoring: Suicidal ideation assessment at every visit
Consider dose adjustment if no response by 6-8 weeks
referral_criteria: Psychiatry: treatment-resistant depression, suicidal ideation, psychotic features, bipolar concern
red_flags: Suicidal ideation with plan or intent

first_line: Anticoagulation based on CHA2DS2-VASc score (>=2 men, >=3 women)
Target heart rate <110 bpm (lenient) or <80 bpm (strict)
second_line: Rhythm control: antiarrhythmic drugs (amiodarone, flecainide, sotalol)
Left atrial appendage occlusion if anticoagulation contraindicated
referral_criteria: Emergency: rapid ventricular response with hemodynamic instability

first_line: SGLT2 inhibitor (dapagliflozin, empagliflozin) for eGFR 20-90 with albuminuria
second_line: Phosphate binders (sevelamer, calcium acetate) for hyperphosphatemia
monitoring: eGFR and urine albumin/creatinine ratio every 3-6 months
referral_criteria: Nephrology: eGFR <30, rapid decline (>5 mL/min/year), uncertain etiology

second_line: Oral NSAIDs (lowest effective dose, shortest duration)
Intra-articular corticosteroid injections (limit to 3-4/year per joint)
monitoring: Pain assessment at each visit (NRS or VAS)
referral_criteria: Orthopedics: failure of conservative management, consideration for joint replacement
red_flags: Signs of inflammatory arthritis (prolonged morning stiffness, multiple joint involvement)

first_line: Step down to H2 blocker (famotidine) or PRN PPI after symptom control
lifestyle_modifications: Avoid trigger foods (caffeine, chocolate, alcohol, spicy/fatty foods)
monitoring: Consider step-down after 8 weeks of PPI
referral_criteria: GI: alarm symptoms (dysphagia, weight loss, bleeding), failure of 8-week PPI trial
Endoscopy: screening for Barrett's if chronic GERD >5 years
Surgical: refractory symptoms despite maximal medical therapy
red_flags: Chest pain (rule out cardiac cause first)

first_line: Oral iron supplementation (ferrous sulfate 325mg daily on empty stomach)
Vitamin C 250-500mg with iron to enhance absorption
second_line: IV iron (ferric carboxymaltose, iron sucrose) if oral intolerant or malabsorption
Blood transfusion if Hgb <7 or symptomatic severe anemia
lifestyle_modifications: Iron-rich diet (red meat, spinach, beans, fortified cereals)
Avoid calcium, tea, coffee with iron supplement (interfere with absorption)
Take iron on empty stomach if tolerated
monitoring: CBC and reticulocyte count at 4-8 weeks
Ferritin at 3 months to assess replenishment
Continue iron 3-6 months after hemoglobin normalizes to replete stores
If no response in 4-6 weeks, reassess diagnosis
referral_criteria: GI: men or postmenopausal women (evaluate for GI bleeding)
Celiac disease screening if malabsorption suspected
red_flags: Concurrent leukopenia or thrombocytopenia (consider bone marrow pathology)
No response to adequate iron trial

first_line: Outpatient (with comorbidities): amoxicillin/clavulanate + macrolide OR respiratory fluoroquinolone
Inpatient (non-ICU): beta-lactam + macrolide OR respiratory fluoroquinolone
Inpatient (ICU): beta-lactam + macrolide OR beta-lactam + respiratory fluoroquinolone
second_line: If MRSA risk: add vancomycin or linezolid
If Pseudomonas risk: piperacillin-tazobactam or cefepime + fluoroquinolone
monitoring: Clinical improvement expected within 48-72 hours of antibiotics
Repeat chest X-ray at 6-8 weeks if high-risk (age >50, smoker) to confirm resolution
referral_criteria: Infectious disease: treatment failure, unusual organisms
Hospital admission: CURB-65 >=2 or PSI Class IV-V
red_flags: Sepsis (hypotension, altered mental status, organ dysfunction)
No improvement after 72h of appropriate antibiotics

first_line: Acute: NSAIDs (ibuprofen, naproxen) for mild-moderate attacks
Acute: Triptans (sumatriptan, rizatriptan) for moderate-severe attacks
Preventive (if >=4 headache days/month): topiramate, propranolol, amitriptyline
monitoring: Headache diary (frequency, severity, triggers, medication use)
Medication overuse monitoring (limit acute meds to <10-15 days/month)
referral_criteria: Neurology: diagnostic uncertainty, treatment failure with 2+ preventive medications
Emergency: thunderclap headache, worst headache of life, new neurological deficits
Headache specialist: chronic daily headache, medication overuse headache

first_line: Levothyroxine replacement (1.6 mcg/kg/day ideal body weight)
Start at full dose in young, healthy patients
Start at lower dose (25-50 mcg) in elderly or cardiac disease patients
second_line: Liothyronine (T3) add-on if persistent symptoms despite normal TSH (controversial)
Desiccated thyroid extract (not preferred by most guidelines)
Brand name levothyroxine if dose sensitivity or inconsistent generic formulations
lifestyle_modifications: Take levothyroxine on empty stomach, 30-60 min before breakfast
Separate from calcium, iron, and PPI by 4 hours
monitoring: Lipid panel (dyslipidemia may improve with treatment)
referral_criteria: Endocrinology: thyroid nodule, suspected central hypothyroidism, pregnancy planning, refractory symptoms

first_line: Uncomplicated cystitis: nitrofurantoin 100mg BID x 5 days
Alternative: TMP-SMX 160/800mg BID x 3 days (if local resistance <20%)
second_line: Fluoroquinolone (ciprofloxacin 250mg BID x 3 days) - reserve for pyelonephritis or complicated UTI
Pyelonephritis: fluoroquinolone x 5-7 days or TMP-SMX x 14 days
monitoring: No test of cure needed for uncomplicated cystitis
Repeat culture if symptoms persist >48h on antibiotics
Post-treatment culture for complicated UTI or pyelonephritis
referral_criteria: Urology: recurrent UTIs (>=3/year), anatomic abnormality, male UTI
Hospital admission: pyelonephritis with sepsis, inability to take oral meds
